skill.name: mobile-games
skill.description: Mobile game development principles. Touch input, battery, performance, app stores.
skill.summary: Essential mobile game development guidance covering touch controls, performance optimization, battery management, and app store requirements.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis false positives: 'LED' in 'OLED' misidentified as weak cryptography, and 'screen/screenshots' misidentified as system reconnaissance. The skill provides mobile game development guidance with no security risks.

content.user_title: Build Mobile Games with Touch Controls
content.value_statement: Mobile game development requires understanding platform constraints like touch input, battery life, and thermal management. This skill provides actionable principles for optimizing mobile games.
actual_capabilities: Design touch-friendly interfaces with proper hit areas and gesture support
Implement thermal management strategies to prevent device overheating
Optimize battery usage through frame rate limiting and resource management
Navigate iOS App Store and Google Play submission requirements
Choose appropriate monetization models for mobile games
Implement responsive UI that supports multiple orientations
limitations: Provides guidance only and does not generate game code or assets
Platform-specific requirements change frequently and may require verification
Does not cover game engine-specific implementation details
Touch control guidelines are general and may need adjustment for specific game genres

use_cases.title: Indie Game Developer
use_cases.description: Plan and optimize your first mobile game with proper touch controls and performance targets.

use_cases.title: Game Studio Team
use_cases.description: Ensure your mobile game meets app store requirements and performance standards before launch.

use_cases.title: Unity/Unreal Developer
use_cases.description: Apply mobile optimization principles when porting desktop games to mobile platforms.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Touch Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the minimum touch target sizes and best practices for mobile game buttons?
prompt_templates.scenario: When designing initial mobile game UI

prompt_templates.title: Performance Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: How should I manage frame rate and thermal throttling for a mobile action game?
prompt_templates.scenario: When optimizing game performance for mobile devices

prompt_templates.title: App Store Compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the current iOS App Store and Google Play requirements for mobile games?
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing game for app store submission

prompt_templates.title: Monetization Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: What monetization model would work best for a casual puzzle game with daily content?

output_examples.input: What are the key touch input principles for mobile games?
output_examples.output: Touch input principles: Use minimum 44x44 point hit areas, provide visual feedback on touch, avoid precise timing requirements, support both portrait and landscape orientations, and design for imprecise input compared to desktop controls.

output_examples.input: How do I optimize my mobile game for battery life?
output_examples.output: Battery optimization strategies: Target 30 FPS instead of 60 FPS when possible, implement sleep mode when paused, minimize GPS and network usage, consider dark mode for OLED devices, and monitor thermal state to throttle performance when device gets warm.

output_examples.input: What app store requirements do I need to meet?
output_examples.output: iOS App Store requires privacy labels, account deletion if accounts exist, and screenshots for all device sizes. Google Play requires targeting the current year's API level, 64-bit support, and recommends using app bundles. Both platforms have specific content and review guidelines.

best_practices: Design touch controls with large hit areas (minimum 44x44 points) to accommodate different finger sizes and screen densities
Monitor device thermal state and dynamically reduce quality or frame rate when the device becomes warm to prevent throttling
Support both portrait and landscape orientations unless your game has a specific reason to lock orientation
anti_patterns: Do not port desktop controls directly to mobile without redesigning for touch input and smaller screens
Do not run at maximum frame rate and quality settings continuously without considering battery drain and device thermals
Do not require constant network connectivity when offline play and cached data synchronization could improve user experience

faq.question: What frame rate should I target for mobile games?
faq.answer: Thirty FPS is often sufficient for mobile games and significantly improves battery life. Reserve sixty FPS for fast-action games where it provides meaningful gameplay benefits.

faq.question: Should I support both portrait and landscape orientations?
faq.answer: Support both orientations unless your game has a specific reason to lock one. Players have different preferences and device usage habits.

faq.question: How do I handle device overheating in my game?
faq.answer: Monitor thermal state and reduce quality settings, limit frame rate, or pause non-essential effects when the device becomes warm. Prevent overheating rather than reacting to it.

faq.question: What monetization model works best for mobile games?
faq.answer: Choose based on your game type: premium for quality games with loyal audiences, free with in-app purchases for casual progression games, ads for hyper-casual high-volume games, and subscriptions for games with regular content updates.

faq.question: Do I need different screenshots for different device sizes?
faq.answer: Yes. iOS App Store requires screenshots for all device sizes. Prepare screenshots for each relevant iPhone and iPad screen size to meet submission requirements.

faq.question: Should I use GPS or location services in my mobile game?
faq.answer: Only if location features are core to gameplay. GPS usage significantly drains battery and requires privacy disclosures. Minimize location updates and consider alternatives.
